summary: PR 2615 attempted to link src/melee/gm/gmopening.c by correcting section ownership and data layout around gmopening. The code change split a previous mixed static struct at .data:0x803DBF78 into explicit rodata Vec3 constants, bss/sbss globals, string literals used directly in sprintf, and a force-active unused float array to preserve data. The config split moved the .bss range 0x80480B38-0x80480D58 from gm_1A4C.c to gmopening.c, and symbols.txt refined gm_803DBF78 from one 0x3C global object into a 0x24 local float object plus two local string labels. The PR body notes that everything matched in objdiff, but marking the file as Matching still caused a data mismatch, so the work is best understood as a partial/linking-data cleanup rather than a fully resolved matching status change.
what_changed: Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt so gm_1A4C.c .bss ends at 0x80480B38 instead of 0x80480D58.
Added a .bss range for melee/gm/gmopening.c from 0x80480B38 to 0x80480D58.
Refined symbols at .data:0x803DBF78: gm_803DBF78 changed from a single 0x3C global object to a 0x24 local float object, with lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 added as 0xC local string labels.
Removed the C-side aggregate struct that combined nine floats and two format strings under gm_803DBF78.
Declared gm_803B7D68 and gm_803B7D74 as static const Vec3 values near the top of gmopening.c with address comments.

Moved gmopening bss/sbss globals near the top of the file with address comments, including gm_80480B38, gm_804D67D0, gm_804D67D4, gm_804D67D8, gm_804D67DC, gm_804D67E0, gm_804D67E1, gm_804D67E2, gm_804D67E4, gm_804D67E8, gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4.
Added a static sdata2_order function that references 82.0f and 290.0f, apparently to influence sdata2 float ordering.
Added a force-active static float array named unused_floats containing the previous nine float values from gm_803DBF78.
Changed fn_801A9FCC to use direct string literals in sprintf calls instead of computing string pointers through a u8 pointer into gm_803DBF78.
Added an address comment to the gm_803DBFB4 static int array.
smart_moves: Separated heterogeneous data that had been modeled as one synthetic struct into symbol-level pieces matching the linker map evidence: float data at gm_803DBF78 and separate string labels at lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8.
Used direct format-string literals in the sprintf calls once the old aggregate struct no longer represented the true data layout cleanly.
Preserved otherwise-unused float data with #pragma force_active, avoiding the compiler dropping data that is needed for section/link matching.
Adjusted splits.txt in tandem with source declarations, which is important when bss ownership crosses file boundaries.
Added explicit address comments on static data declarations, making future section-ordering and symbol-audit work easier.
Introduced a small sdata2_order function to force or document ordering of floating constants 82.0f and 290.0f in .sdata2.
decomp_lessons: Objdiff text/function matching is not sufficient for marking a file Matching when linked data sections still disagree; verify data, bss, sbss, rodata, and sdata2 ownership as well.
Avoid overfitting unknown data as a single C struct when the symbol file and references suggest separate objects or labels; splitting objects can improve link accuracy.
When moving bss symbols between source files, update config/GALE01/splits.txt so each file owns the correct address interval.
String labels embedded after float data may need to be modeled as separate data symbols rather than fields of a synthetic struct.
Force-active pragmas can be used to keep required but unreferenced data emitted for matching, but this should be treated as a deliberate matching tactic.
Address comments on data declarations are useful breadcrumbs for maintaining order-sensitive decompilation source.
naming_conventions: Unknown game-mode data symbols continue to use address-derived gm_XXXXXXXX names, such as gm_803DBF78 and gm_80480B38.
Local anonymous data/string labels may use lbl_XXXXXXXX names, as with lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8.
Order-forcing helper functions may be named descriptively after their purpose, as with sdata2_order.
Temporary or synthetic retained data can be named by role when its real semantic name is unknown, as with unused_floats.
Static data declarations in this file use address comments of the form /* 3B7D68 */ or /* 4D67D0 */ before the declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push, #pragma force_active on, and #pragma pop around unused_floats to force emission of otherwise-unused float data.
Added sdata2_order with casts to void for 82.0f and 290.0f to affect or preserve ordering of .sdata2 constants.
Replaced pointer arithmetic into a synthetic data blob with direct string literals to better model the compiler's emitted string references.
Moved declarations earlier in source to match section ordering for rodata, bss, sbss, and data.
Changed symbols.txt object sizes and scopes to reflect the intended split: a 0x24 local float object followed by two 0xC local string labels.
Changed splits.txt bss boundaries to attach gm_80480B38 and subsequent bss range to gmopening.c rather than gm_1A4C.c.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly warns that although everything matches in objdiff, marking the file as Matching causes a data mismatch; evidence for the underlying cause is not provided in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Why does gmopening still produce a data mismatch when marked Matching despite objdiff matching?
Audit gmopening.c section ordering for .data, .rodata, .bss, .sbss, and .sdata2 after PR 2615.
Check whether unused_floats and sdata2_order are still necessary or can be replaced by cleaner semantic declarations.
Verify whether lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 should remain local string labels or receive semantic names once gmopening behavior is better understood.
Review neighboring gm_1A4C.c bss ownership after moving 0x80480B38-0x80480D58 to gmopening.c.
